jokes.text: God!
jokes.setup: Episode opens with someone in apparent distress
jokes.punchline: Single word of exasperation establishing chaos
jokes.explanation: Simple opening beat that establishes chaos but lacks comedic structure

jokes.text: Those are some interesting marks on your neck. Let me guess. Ex-wife's getting remarried, you get a little jealous.
jokes.setup: Detective notices marks on Dennis's neck and makes assumption about domestic violence
jokes.punchline: The detective's assumption is completely wrong - Dennis wanted the wedding to succeed
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and ironic setup for Dennis's actual motivation

jokes.text: Why is Maureen getting married in the middle of the woods? / It's a monster day.
jokes.setup: Mac asks reasonable question about woods wedding
jokes.punchline: Charlie's non-sequitur about Friday the 13th being 'monster day'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his unique logic and delivery of 'monster day' is both absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: Just don't like getting bussed up there. I feel like we're gonna be trapped.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks why they couldn't let him go alone
jokes.punchline: Frank's paranoid concern about being trapped by public transportation
jokes.explanation: Shows Frank's paranoia and contributes to the characters being terrible at planning

jokes.text: I'm tired of paying alimony.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he wants the wedding to happen
jokes.punchline: Reduces his ex-wife's happiness to a financial inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - reducing human relationships to personal financial impact

jokes.text: You know why? 'Cause it's Friday the 13th.
jokes.setup: Observation about lots of bats in the woods
jokes.punchline: Charlie's superstitious explanation connecting bats to Friday the 13th
jokes.explanation: Another good Charlie moment showing his superstitious worldview

jokes.text: Jesus Christ! You guys crashing my wedding? Wait. What?! You?! Wait, Liam, you're marrying Maureen?
jokes.setup: Dennis discovers someone at the wedding
jokes.punchline: His shock at discovering it's Liam McPoyle marrying his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Good escalating revelation and Dennis's genuine shock

jokes.text: Big-time. Don't be jelly.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks if Liam is really marrying Maureen
jokes.punchline: Liam's casual confirmation with 'don't be jelly' (jealous)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Liam's casual weirdness and the dated slang

jokes.text: Oldest angle in the book, my friend. Love.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks what Liam's angle is
jokes.punchline: Liam deadpan delivers 'love' as if it's a scheme
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - treating love like a con game, perfect economy and delivery

jokes.text: That's a negative, Ronald McDonald.
jokes.setup: Question about Ryan's approval
jokes.punchline: Liam calling someone 'Ronald McDonald' as an insult
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly specific and weird insult that fits Liam's character perfectly

jokes.text: See you bitches on the dance floor.
jokes.setup: Liam ending the conversation
jokes.punchline: His incongruously aggressive/casual sign-off about dancing
jokes.explanation: Great Liam line - mixing aggression with wedding pleasantries

jokes.text: I'm here to support my friend, Bill Ponderosa. I'm his AA sponsor!
jokes.setup: Questioned about presence at wedding
jokes.punchline: Frank being an AA sponsor is inherently ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Frank as AA sponsor is perfect character irony - alcoholic helping alcoholic

jokes.text: I just got tagged by a bat! He got you. I got tagged! Suck out the poison, Dee!
jokes.setup: Frank gets hit by a bat
jokes.punchline: His panicked overreaction demanding Dee suck out non-existent poison
jokes.explanation: Great escalating physical comedy with Frank's typical overreaction

jokes.text: Let's go break up this wedding.
jokes.setup: Chaos at wedding location
jokes.punchline: Dee switching to active sabotage
jokes.explanation: Dee's immediate pivot to chaos is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I'll give you $200, you suck it out. $200? I'll do it!
jokes.setup: Frank offers money for Dee to suck out poison
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately volunteers when money is mentioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his willingness to do anything gross for money

jokes.text: Did you swallow it?! Of course I swallowed. Make yourself throw up!
jokes.setup: Charlie sucked Frank's head
jokes.punchline: Frank's horror that Charlie swallowed the 'poison' and needs to vomit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd bat poison scenario

jokes.text: Let's go break up this wedding. Stop, stop! So you were there to break up the wedding. Shit. Did I just say that part?
jokes.setup: Dee suggests breaking up the wedding
jokes.punchline: Dennis accidentally confessing in front of the detective
jokes.explanation: Good narrative structure joke - Dennis's slip-up advances the interrogation

jokes.text: The McPoyle bloodline's been pure and clean for a thousand years. This marriage is an abomination.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining opposition to wedding
jokes.punchline: Incest pride presented as noble tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect McPoyle moment - proud of genetic isolation

jokes.text: The McPoyle bloodline's been pure and clean for a thousand years. / When you say 'pure and clean,' you mean what, exactly? / Means we haven't bred outside the bloodline.
jokes.setup: Ryan talks about pure bloodline
jokes.punchline: The revelation that it means incest for a thousand years
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - the casual admission of generational incest delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: For a thousand years? Yeah. That makes a lot of sense.
jokes.setup: Confirmation of thousand years of incest
jokes.punchline: Charlie's deadpan 'that makes a lot of sense'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to insane information - great economy

jokes.text: Okay, I brought a disguise. Meh! They're gonna know who you are.
jokes.setup: Ryan claims to have brought a disguise
jokes.punchline: The disguise is apparently terrible, dismissed immediately
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about an inadequate disguise

jokes.text: Now sneak me in so we can go kill Maureen. Kill? Who said anything about killing anybody?
jokes.setup: Ryan wants to be snuck in
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of killing Maureen shocks Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ryan's psychotic tendencies vs Dennis's limits

jokes.text: Well, I feel it would be best if I pounded a beer. I'll support that decision.
jokes.setup: Seeking sobriety advice
jokes.punchline: Frank encouraging drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Frank completely misunderstanding sponsor role

jokes.text: You're my sponsor, Frank. Come on. My support system. Right, right, right. Of course. So, do whatever you feel is best.
jokes.setup: Bill reminds Frank he's his AA sponsor
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately gives terrible sponsor advice
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Frank as the worst possible sponsor

jokes.text: Well, I feel it would be best if I pounded a beer. I'll support that decision.
jokes.setup: Bill wants to drink at his triggering wedding
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately supporting the worst decision for a recovering alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy about the worst AA sponsor ever - perfect character integration

jokes.text: I mean, it's a dry wedding, for God's sakes. They're serving milk.
jokes.setup: Bill complaining about the dry wedding
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail that they're serving milk instead of alcohol
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail - serving milk at a wedding is wonderfully weird

jokes.text: I feel like maybe I should do some drugs then, you know? That bad? You earned it.
jokes.setup: Bill suggests drugs as alternative to alcohol
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately encouraging drug use too
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Frank being the worst sponsor - 'you earned it' is hilariously wrong

jokes.text: You're a bad sponsor. Why? I supported every decision he wanted to make.
jokes.setup: Dee calls Frank a bad sponsor
jokes.punchline: Frank's defense completely misses the point of sponsorship
jokes.explanation: Excellent joke about Frank completely misunderstanding what being supportive means

jokes.text: Dude, are you thinking what I'm thinking? Yeah. We could definitely get laid here.
jokes.setup: Mac asks if Charlie's thinking the same thing
jokes.punchline: Charlie's priority is getting laid at a disaster wedding
jokes.explanation: Shows the characters' misplaced priorities during chaos

jokes.text: Yeah, I realize my timing is a little unorthodox, but, I figure what better way to make sure your union is totally complete than by having you sign these documents that I need you to sign?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to get Maureen to sign papers at her wedding
jokes.punchline: His transparently selfish framing as being for her benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis manipulation - trying to frame his selfish needs as helping her

jokes.text: You big, giant new titties, Maureen! Why would you do that now? Why not when we were together when I could've...?
jokes.setup: Dennis commenting on Maureen's surgery
jokes.punchline: Making her body changes about his missed opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - making her choices about him

jokes.text: I'm talking about your tits! You big, giant new titties, Maureen! Why would you do that now? Why not when we were together when I could've...?
jokes.setup: Dennis notices Maureen has enhanced herself
jokes.punchline: His crude, selfish reaction about timing of her breast augmentation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Dennis moment - making her body modifications about him

jokes.text: What is that all about? I'll tell you what that's all about. Zombies.
jokes.setup: Mac asks about Pappy McPoyle's strange behavior
jokes.punchline: Charlie's immediate jump to 'zombies' as explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie logic leap - going straight to supernatural explanation

jokes.text: Once one gets a taste for its own kind, it can spread through the pack like a wildfire. Mindlessly chomping and biting at their own hinds.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining zombie theory
jokes.punchline: Detailed cannibalism description with animal behavior terminology
jokes.explanation: Charlie treating humans like pack animals is wonderfully weird

jokes.text: You know the thing about a rat? It's got life in its eyes. Black eyes like a doll's eye.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining zombie behavior in rats
jokes.punchline: He's doing the shark speech from Jaws but about rats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to famous Jaws speech but adapted for Charlie's rat obsession - very creative

jokes.text: Are you doing the speech from Jaws? Are you doing Jaws? We don't have time for this shit.
jokes.setup: Charlie doing the Jaws speech
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately recognizing and calling out the reference
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Mac breaking the fourth wall by recognizing the reference

jokes.text: The McPoyles sprung from my loins, fully formed! One of them babies tried to eat me! I ate him first!
jokes.setup: Pappy McPoyle talking about his offspring
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of cannibalistic infanticide
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark and shocking - perfectly captures the McPoyle family's insanity

jokes.text: Syphilis killed about half of us. Mongoloidism got most of the rest.
jokes.setup: Discussing McPoyle family history
jokes.punchline: Casual listing of genetic disasters from inbreeding
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of horrific family medical history. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Syphilis killed about half of us. Mongoloidism got most of the rest.
jokes.setup: Question about what happened to the McPoyle bloodline
jokes.punchline: Casual listing of genetic consequences of inbreeding
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny matter-of-fact explanation of genetic deterioration

jokes.text: And we never saw Dee again. No. We saw Dee. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Dennis dramatically claiming Dee disappeared
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction by other characters
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dennis's dramatic storytelling

jokes.text: She's infected! She's infected!
jokes.setup: Dee appears acting strangely
jokes.punchline: Charlie and Mac's panicked 'zombie infection' diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Continues the zombie misunderstanding storyline

jokes.text: It's not our blood that's being tainted. It's us tainting the blood of others. McPoyles will take over the world!
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining his change of heart about the marriage
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic that McPoyles will dominate through interbreeding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted McPoyle logic - turning contamination into conquest strategy

jokes.text: Remember when Dee, you know, she sucked that bat poison or whatever out of Frank's head from the vampire bat? / Why would a vampire bat turn you into a zombie? You're mixing up your horror genres.
jokes.setup: Mac theorizing about Dee's transformation
jokes.punchline: Charlie being a pedantic horror movie expert
jokes.explanation: Great meta joke about horror genre conventions - Charlie being unexpectedly knowledgeable

jokes.text: You're mixing up your horror genres. If anything, it would turn you into a vampire. Right or a bat, or a bat. Or Batman.
jokes.setup: Discussing vampire bat effects
jokes.punchline: Mac adding Batman to transformation options
jokes.explanation: Mac's innocent addition of Batman to supernatural transformations

jokes.text: That can't possibly be what Batman's about. It's about a grown man who dresses up like a bat? / These are Academy Award winning movies. This is serious stuff.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Batman
jokes.punchline: Charlie's defense of Batman as serious Academy Award material
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Charlie moment - treating superhero movies as high art

jokes.text: Well, you know the Academy, Charlie, I mean... / I know the Academy as well as anybody. / Sometimes, it's atrocious.
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting the Academy makes mistakes
jokes.punchline: Charlie claiming insider knowledge of Academy politics
jokes.explanation: Funny character moment with Charlie pretending to be an industry insider

jokes.text: I did something bad. I did something really, really bad; guys, we got to get out of here before everybody finds out what happened.
jokes.setup: Dennis admits something went wrong with Maureen
jokes.punchline: His panic about getting caught before revealing what he did
jokes.explanation: Good tension building - Dennis's guilt without revealing the specifics yet

jokes.text: Here, have a sip, right from the cow's titty. You'll love it. / How is drinking a glass of milk going to loosen me up, Bill? / Well, I spiked the milk with methylenedioxypyrovalerone.
jokes.setup: Bill offering Dee milk to relax
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that he spiked the milk with dangerous drugs
jokes.explanation: Great dark revelation - explains the 'zombie' behavior with dangerous drug cocktail

jokes.text: It's bath salts. Yeah, yeah, it's like coke and acid and meth all combined. It's awesome.
jokes.setup: Explanation of what the drug is
jokes.punchline: Bill's enthusiastic description of the dangerous drug combination
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bill's reckless enthusiasm for dangerous substances

jokes.text: Well, I spiked the milk with methylenedioxypyrovalerone. What? It's bath salts. Yeah, yeah, it's like coke and acid and meth all combined.
jokes.setup: Bill offering milk to Dee
jokes.punchline: Revelation about spiking wedding refreshments with dangerous drug
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation - explains all the chaos with one horrible decision. Bill's casual drug knowledge is perfect.

jokes.text: Dude, when was the last time you drove a car? Don't worry about it, man. I can drive; I remember how. / What happened, dude? / I forgot how to drive.
jokes.setup: Question about Charlie's driving ability
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confidence immediately followed by admission he forgot
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie's incompetence and overconfidence

jokes.text: Dude, when was the last time you drove a car? Don't worry about it, man. I can drive; I remember how. What happened, dude? I forgot how to drive.
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming he can drive
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission of forgetting basic skill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - confident incompetence immediately revealed

jokes.text: You sons of bitches! You're gonna pay! You're all gonna pay! I'll kill you! / No, we won't pay. We never do.
jokes.setup: Dee's rage-filled threats about paying
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact response about never paying for anything
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response - Charlie completely missing the point and being accidentally profound about their characters

jokes.text: Since you're the one with motive and the last one to be seen with her, you're my new #1 suspect.
jokes.setup: Police officer wrapping up investigation
jokes.punchline: Dennis becoming prime suspect in missing person case
jokes.explanation: Good escalation putting Dennis in real jeopardy

jokes.text: God, Maureen. That was terrible. Who did those tits? The nipple placement is crazy.
jokes.setup: Dennis after sleeping with Maureen
jokes.punchline: His immediate criticism of her plastic surgery post-coitus
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Dennis moment - critiquing her body immediately after sex, showing his complete narcissism

jokes.text: Never. Let's run away together. I'm gonna run away, but not with you.
jokes.setup: Maureen suggesting elopement
jokes.punchline: Dennis's brutal honesty about fleeing alone
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel Dennis response - taking her suggestion but excluding her entirely

jokes.text: You expect me to believe this woman's gonna throw her entire life away just to sleep with her ex-husband on her wedding night? Really?
jokes.setup: Detective hearing Dennis's story
jokes.punchline: His incredulous reaction to the implausible story
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to the absurd situation

jokes.text: Come on, silly head. Let's go home and put this whole adventure behind us. / No. No! No, you got to help me. What have I done?
jokes.setup: Maureen appearing to bail Dennis out
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horror at being trapped with her forever
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Dennis's plan completely backfired and he's trapped forever
